lint.detail: Warnings only: dead_code in test helpers (paging.rs constants, cross_module.rs fields, adversarial test models). No errors. Pre-existing warnings, not introduced by this milestone.

variable-font-files.summary: Variable fonts integrated (Nunito Sans 4-axis 569KB, Source Code Pro wght 212KB), init loading updated, glyph_id u8→u16 truncation fix, 12 tests covering VAL-VARFONT-001 and VAL-VARFONT-004.

variable-font-axis-rendering.summary: Axis-aware rasterization with gvar interpolation, HarfRust Variation/ShaperInstance shaping, cache key extended to 3-tuple with axis_hash, TextRun.axis_hash for scene graph flow. 11 new tests, covers VAL-VARFONT-002/003, VAL-CACHE-005, VAL-CROSS-002.

font-fallback-chain.summary: FallbackChain struct with cluster-based fallback resolution, content-type-aware font ordering, font_identifier_hash for cache separation. 16 tests covering VAL-FALLBACK-001/002/003/004, VAL-CACHE-004.

unicode-glyph-coverage.summary: 22 tests proving pipeline handles Latin Extended, scene graph round-trip for Unicode text, and supplementary plane codepoints. No impl changes needed. Covers VAL-UNICODE-001/002/003.

misc-text-shaping-cleanup.summary: bytes_to_shaped_glyphs deduplicated into scene library, SAFETY comments added to all unsafe blocks, drawing/lib.rs doc updated, dead types removed from truetype.rs.

nonBlockingIssues.description: Old static font files (nunito-sans.ttf, source-code-pro.ttf) remain in system/share/ alongside variable versions. Used as test fixtures but no longer needed at runtime.
Code duplication: rasterize_with_axes() duplicates ~120 lines from rasterize(). Could share inner function. Tests confirm correctness.
SAFETY comment on push_text_runs (scene/lib.rs:540) incorrectly claims 'no padding' but TextRun has 2 bytes internal padding. Pre-existing inaccuracy, not introduced by this feature.

VAL-FALLBACK-002 tests don't genuinely exercise fallback-to-secondary font path — both test fonts share broad Latin coverage so primary always resolves. The fallback logic is correct (cluster-based matching is sound) but the tests don't fully prove secondary font resolution with real font data.

Whole-text reshaping for per-glyph fallback: when one glyph needs fallback, entire text is reshaped with each fallback font. Acceptable for current use case (short strings), but scalability concern for longer documents.
Unused include_bytes! constants (NUNITO_SANS, SOURCE_CODE_PRO) in unicode.rs and fallback.rs generate compiler warnings and embed ~147KB dead data per test file.

appliedUpdates.description: Added 'Glyph Cache Key Convention' section to .factory/library/font-rendering.md documenting FNV-1a hashing pattern used in axis_values_hash() and font_identifier_hash(), cache key composition via XOR into axis_hash field.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: font-rendering-worker skill should clarify when QEMU visual verification is required vs optional. Library-only features that add infrastructure (e.g., axis rendering API) without changing the active rendering path don't need QEMU screenshots. Condition: 'mandatory when the feature changes compositor rendering or scene graph consumption code; optional when changes are library-only and not yet wired to active display path.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: variable-font-axis-rendering worker correctly skipped QEMU testing since axis_hash values are all 0 in current compositor. The skill mandates visual verification for all display pipeline features without distinguishing infrastructure from active-path changes.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: font-rendering-worker skill should mention that public API signature changes in shared libraries (e.g., GlyphCache.get()) require updating all callers including test files, not just the primary consumer.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: variable-font-files worker had to fix 4 test call sites in drawing.rs after changing GlyphCache.get() signature from u8 to u16.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: font-rendering-worker skill should have a cleanup/refactoring mode that makes TDD and QEMU visual verification steps optional for non-behavioral changes.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: misc-text-shaping-cleanup worker noted TDD and QEMU steps were inapplicable for pure refactoring. Worker correctly skipped them but had to document the deviation.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add guidance for cross-cutting test files: 'Cross-cutting feature tests that span multiple subsystems (shaping, scene graph, cache, rasterization) may use a dedicated test file named for the feature or domain (e.g., unicode.rs, fallback.rs) rather than forcing tests into subsystem-specific files.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: unicode-glyph-coverage and font-fallback-chain both created feature-scoped test files spanning multiple subsystems. AGENTS.md only mentions subsystem-specific test files (shaping.rs, drawing.rs, scene.rs).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: 'When modifying #[repr(C)] structs used for cross-process shared memory serialization, verify SAFETY comments accurately reflect padding. Use core::mem::size_of::<T>() assertions and field offset checks to document actual layout.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: SAFETY comment in scene/lib.rs:540 claims 'no padding' for TextRun but struct has 2 bytes internal padding between font_size and axis_hash. Inaccuracy pre-dates this milestone but highlights a gap in struct layout documentation practices.

rejectedObservations.observation: Convention needed for superseded binary assets (old static fonts vs new variable fonts in system/share/)
rejectedObservations.reason: Too narrow — this is a single-project, single-instance concern. The old fonts are correctly kept as test fixtures. Not worth a cross-project convention.

rejectedObservations.observation: AGENTS.md doesn't mention convention against unused test data imports (include_bytes! for fonts not used in tests)
rejectedObservations.reason: Compiler warnings already surface this. Adding a convention would be redundant with Rust's built-in dead_code warnings.

rejectedObservations.observation: Pre-existing tech debt in rasterizer.rs (#[allow(dead_code)] annotations on most types/functions)
rejectedObservations.reason: Outside milestone scope. Already logged as discoveredIssue in misc-text-shaping-cleanup handoff. Will be addressed when drawing library's rasterizer.rs is assessed for removal (the canonical rasterizer is now in shaping/src/rasterize.rs).
